query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Infer', 'To deduce or conclude from evidence and reasoning', '“From her tone, he infers that she was upset.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inference', 'The act or process of inferring by deduction or induction', 'Her inference became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infernal', 'Hellish, fiendish, diabolical', 'Her infernal became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infest', 'To live in or overrun to an unwanted degree', 'The essay highlights the infest of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infidel', 'A non-believer of a certain religion', 'Scholars noted a striking infidel between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infiltrate', 'To surreptitiously penetrate, enter or gain access', 'Over time, the community learned to infiltrate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infinite', 'Indefinably large, countlessly great, immense', 'The essay highlights the infinite of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infinitesimal', 'Incalculably, exceedingly, or immeasurably minute', 'Scholars noted a striking infinitesimal between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infirm', 'To contradict, to provide proof that something is not', 'Critics argued that the plan might infirm the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infirmary', 'A place where sick or injured people are cared for', 'Scholars noted a striking infirmary between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infirmity', 'Feebleness, frailty or ailment, especially due to old age', 'The author hoped the new policy would infirmity tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inflammable', 'Capable of burning, easily set on fire', 'The reviewer described the tone as inflammable, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inflammatory', 'Tending to inflame or provoke somebody', 'Over time, the community learned to inflammatory its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inflexible', 'Not flexible, not capable of bending or being bent, stiff', 'The passage presents a inflexible portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Influenc', 'Having an effect on the character, development, or behavior of someone or something', 'The reviewer described the tone as influenc, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Influence', 'The capacity to be a compelling force on actions, behavior', 'Her influence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Influx', 'A flow inward or into something', 'The essay highlights the influx of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infraction', 'A minor offence, petty crime', 'The essay highlights the infraction of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infrastructure', 'The basic, underlying framework of a system of organization', 'Her infrastructure became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infrequent', 'Rarely happening or present, only occasional', 'The reviewer described the tone as infrequent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infringe', 'Break or violate a treaty, a law, a right etc', 'The essay highlights the infringe of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infuse', 'To cause to become an element of something, insert or fill', 'Over time, the community learned to infuse its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infusion', 'That which is infused', 'The essay highlights the infusion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ingenious', 'Cleverly inventive or resourceful; marked by originality and creativity', 'The reviewer described the tone as ingenious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ingenue', 'An innocent, naïve, wholesome girl or young woman', 'Scholars noted a striking ingenue between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ingénue', 'Alternative spelling of ingénue', 'Her ingénue became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ingenuous', 'Innocent and unsuspecting', 'What seemed ingenuous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inglorious', 'Ignominious, disgraceful, not famous, obscure', 'The reviewer described the tone as inglorious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ingraft', 'To insert, as of one plant into another, for propagation', 'Critics argued that the plan might ingraft the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ingrate', 'Ungrateful', 'Over time, the community learned to ingrate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ingratiate', 'To establish (oneself) in the favor or good graces of others', 'The author hoped the new policy would ingratiate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ingratitude', 'A lack or absence of gratitude, thanklessness', 'The essay highlights the ingratitude of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inherent', 'Existing as a natural or essential characteristic', '“The risks of surgery are inherent to the procedure.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inhibit', 'To hinder, restrain, or prevent an action', 'Over time, the community learned to inhibit its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inhibitory', 'Serving to slow down or restrain a process', '“The drug has an inhibitory effect on nerve signals.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inhospitable', 'Not inclined to hospitality, unfriendly', 'The author hoped the new policy would inhospitable tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inhuman', 'Of or pertaining to inhumanity and the indifferently cruel', 'The author hoped the new policy would inhuman tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inhume', 'To bury in a grave', 'The author hoped the new policy would inhume tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inimical', 'Tending to obstruct or harm', 'Critics argued that the plan might inimical the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inimitable', 'Beyond imitation, surpassing all others, matchless', 'What seemed inimitable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Iniquitous', 'Characterized by injustice or wickedness', 'The reviewer described the tone as iniquitous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Iniquity', 'Deviation from what is right, wickedness, gross injustice', 'The essay highlights the iniquity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Initiate', 'To begin, to start, to introduce', 'Critics argued that the plan might initiate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inject', 'To pump (especially fluids) into a cavity or passage', 'The author hoped the new policy would inject tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Injunction', 'An authoritative order', 'The essay highlights the injunction of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inkl', 'A slight suspicion or hint', 'Her inkl became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inland', 'Within the land, remote from the ocean', 'The essay highlights the inland of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inlet', 'An indentation of a shoreline, small bay or arm', 'Her inlet became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inmost', 'The very deepest within, innermost', 'The essay highlights the inmost of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Innate', 'Existing in one from birth, inborn, native', 'Critics argued that the plan might innate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
